digest: Today's biggest story is Anthropic's lawsuit against the Pentagon over being labeled a 'supply chain risk,' sparking debate about AI company-government relations. Meanwhile, Claude Code rolled out significant updates including automatic memory management, recurring task scheduling with /loop commands, and Opus 4.6 now defaults to medium effort for better performance.

stories.headline: Anthropic Sues Pentagon Over 'Supply Chain Risk' Blacklisting
stories.summary: Anthropic has filed a lawsuit against the U.S. Defense Department challenging its designation as a 'supply chain risk' [1][2][3][4]. The legal action aims to block Pentagon blacklisting over AI use restrictions [4]. The case has generated significant discussion in tech circles, with multiple major outlets covering the unprecedented move by an AI company against federal classification [1][2][3][4].
key_points: First major AI company to legally challenge government risk designation
Lawsuit targets Pentagon's supplier restriction policies
Case highlights growing tensions between AI companies and defense agencies

sources.title: Anthropic sues US Government for calling it a risk
Anthropic Sues Pentagon over 'Supply Chain Risk' Label
Anthropic sues US defense department over blacklisting
Anthropic sues to block Pentagon blacklisting over AI use restrictions

stories.headline: Anthropic Opens Fourth Office in Sydney for Asia Pacific Expansion
stories.summary: Anthropic has announced the opening of its fourth office location in Sydney, Australia, marking the company's continued expansion into the Asia Pacific region [1]. This represents a significant milestone in Anthropic's global growth strategy.

stories.headline: Anthropic Partners with Mozilla on Firefox Security Initiative
stories.summary: Anthropic has announced a new partnership with Mozilla focused on Firefox security [1]. The collaboration appears to be part of Anthropic's broader efforts to work with major technology companies on security and safety initiatives.

stories.headline: Claude Code Introduces Automatic Memory Management and Interactive Copy Features
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.59 launched automatic memory saving where Claude stores useful context that users can manage with /memory [1]. The update also added an interactive /copy command that displays a picker for selecting individual code blocks or full responses when multiple code blocks are present [1].
key_points: Auto-memory automatically saves useful context during sessions
Interactive /copy picker for selecting specific code blocks
Improved bash command prefix suggestions for compound commands

stories.headline: Claude Code Adds Recurring Task Scheduling with /loop Command
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.71 introduced the /loop command for running prompts or slash commands on recurring intervals, such as '/loop 5m check the deploy' [1]. The update also added cron scheduling tools for recurring prompts within sessions and a rebindable voice activation key [1].
key_points: /loop command enables scheduled recurring tasks
Built-in cron scheduling for session-based automation
Customizable voice activation keybinding with zero typing interference

stories.headline: Opus 4.6 Now Defaults to Medium Effort for Better Performance Balance
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.68 set Opus 4.6 to default to medium effort for Max and Team subscribers, which provides a sweet spot between speed and thoroughness [1]. Users can still change effort levels with /model, and the 'ultrathink' keyword enables high effort for individual turns [1]. Older Opus versions (4 and 4.1) have been removed from the first-party API [1].
key_points: Medium effort becomes new default for optimal speed-quality balance
'Ultrathink' keyword available for high-effort individual responses
Legacy Opus models automatically migrated to 4.6

stories.headline: Claude Code Adds Enhanced File Operations and SSH Support
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.72 introduced the 'w' key in /copy to write focused selections directly to files, bypassing the clipboard for better SSH workflows [1]. The update also added an optional description argument to /plan for immediate planning mode activation and an ExitWorktree tool for leaving EnterWorktree sessions [1].
key_points: Direct file writing from /copy bypasses clipboard for SSH users
/plan command accepts descriptions for immediate planning
ExitWorktree tool for better worktree session management

stories.headline: Claude Code Resolves API Compatibility Issues with Third-Party Gateways
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.70 fixed API 400 errors when using ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL with third-party gateways by correctly detecting proxy endpoints and disabling tool_reference blocks [1]. The update also resolved issues with custom Bedrock inference profiles and empty model responses after ToolSearch [1].
key_points: Fixed compatibility with third-party API gateways and proxies
Resolved Bedrock custom inference profile errors
Improved tool search reliability after system operations

stories.headline: Claude Code Introduces Claude API Skill and Remote Control Enhancements
stories.summary: Claude Code v2.1.69 added the /claude-api skill for building applications with the Claude API and Anthropic SDK [1]. The update also improved bash mode navigation with Ctrl+U support and enhanced remote control sessions with custom naming options [1].
key_points: New /claude-api skill for API development workflows
Enhanced keyboard navigation in bash mode
Custom session naming for remote control functionality

stories.headline: Community Analysis Challenges Claims About Claude Code Operating Costs
stories.summary: A detailed community analysis disputed claims that Anthropic spends $5,000 per Claude Code user [1]. The blog post generated significant discussion on Hacker News with 451 points and 326 comments, suggesting the community found the cost analysis compelling and worth debating [1].
key_points: Independent analysis questions widely cited Claude Code cost figures
Strong community engagement with 451 points and 326 comments
Demonstrates community interest in understanding AI economics

sources.title: No, it doesn't cost Anthropic $5k per Claude Code user

stories.headline: Use @-mentions to give Claude Code precise file context
stories.summary: Boris Cherny, creator of Claude Code, recommends using @-mentions to point Claude at specific files instead of letting it search. This dramatically reduces token usage and improves accuracy since Claude works with the exact files you intend rather than guessing from project structure.
actionable_steps: Type @ in Claude Code to see a file picker with fuzzy search
Select the specific files relevant to your task before writing your prompt
Combine multiple @-mentions for cross-file tasks like refactoring

stories.headline: Write a CLAUDE.md file to set project conventions once
stories.summary: Adding a CLAUDE.md to your project root lets you define coding standards, test commands, and architectural decisions that persist across every Claude Code session. Think of it as onboarding documentation that Claude reads automatically — you teach it once instead of repeating yourself.
actionable_steps: Create a CLAUDE.md in your project root with build/test/lint commands
Add coding conventions like naming patterns, preferred libraries, and file structure
Include architectural decisions and constraints Claude should respect
Use /init in Claude Code to auto-generate a starter CLAUDE.md

stories.headline: Use Claude's extended thinking for complex architectural decisions
stories.summary: When asking Claude to make non-trivial design choices — like choosing between patterns, structuring a new module, or planning a migration — enabling extended thinking produces significantly better results. Claude will reason through trade-offs before committing to an approach, catching issues that quick responses miss.
actionable_steps: For architecture questions, ask Claude to 'think through the trade-offs' explicitly
Use Claude Code's plan mode (shift+tab) for multi-step tasks that need careful reasoning
Review Claude's reasoning in extended thinking to catch flawed assumptions early

sources.title: Tips for getting better architectural advice from Claude
